title: Postdoctoral Scholarship (2 years) within computational modeling of intracellular signaling
description:
Umeå University is one of Sweden’s largest higher education institutions with over 37,000 students and about 4,700 employees. The University offers a diversity of high-quality education and world-leading research in several fields. Notably, the groundbreaking discovery of the CRISPR-Cas9 gene-editing tool, which was awarded the Nobel Prize in Chemistry, was made here. At Umeå University, everything is close. Our cohesive campuses make it easy to meet, work together and exchange knowledge, which promotes a dynamic and open culture. The ongoing societal transformation and large green investments in northern Sweden create enormous opportunities and complex challenges. For Umeå University, conducting research about – and in the middle of – a society in transition is key. We also take pride in delivering education to enable regions to expand quickly and sustainably. In fact, the future is made here. Are you interested in learning more? Read here


The Faculty of Medicine, which consists of 12 departments, is responsible for biomedical research and courses in the field of nursing and health care and has an extensive research and graduate education in more than 80 subjects.

**Postdoctoral** **Scholarship (2 years) within computational modeling of intracellular signaling**

The *Department of Medical Biosciences* is offering a postdoctoral scholarship within the project “Developing computational tools for large-scale human intracellular signaling models”. The scholarship is full-time for 2 years, with access starting in September 2026 or by agreement.

**Departmental specific information**


The research will be carried out in the laboratory of Cemal Erdem at the Department of Medical Biosciences, which offers an international, collaborative, and open-minded research environment. Please visit the lab’s webpage for more information: https://erdemlab.github.io.


The Erdem research group is part of the SciLifeLab and Wallenberg National Program for Data-Driven Life Science (DDLS), which aims to recruit and train the next-generation of data-driven life scientists and to create globally leading computational and data science capabilities in Sweden. The program is funded with a total of 3.3 billion SEK over 12 years from the Knut and Alice Wallenberg (KAW) Foundation. DDLS uses data, computational methods and artificial intelligence to study biological systems and processes at all levels, from molecular structures and cellular processes to human health and global ecosystems. The incumbent will be part of this research ecosystem.

**Project description**


A key missing capability in current cancer research is the ability to predict how a particular cancer cell will respond to a perturbation (e.g., drug inhibition or gene knockdown). However, building computational models of complex, large-scale, and incompletely understood systems remains extremely challenging. To address this issue, we recently developed the SPARCED pipeline to convert structured lists of species, parameters, and reaction types into an SBML (Systems Biology Markup Language) model file, and we created a model based on one of the largest pan-cancer signaling models in the literature.


SPARCED is compatible with high-performance and cloud computing, can simulate thousands to millions of single-cell trajectories, is easily expandable with new pathways, and is retrainable with new omics data for new cellular contexts. In this project, we will further develop the SPARCED pipeline for: (i) even larger-scale model construction, (ii) training personalized computational models in new contexts, and (iii) studying in-silico clinical intervention strategies.


The postdoctoral fellow will have the opportunity to:

* Learn about computational modeling pipelines for human intracellular signaling pathways
* Develop our SPARCED pipeline further
* Construct mechanistic models to study *in silico* drug combination responses in cancer cells
* Construct mechanistic models to replicate 2D-3D tissue architectures


In our lab, we are committed to fostering a collaborative and inclusive environment that promotes reproducible and rigorous research practices. As a postdoctoral fellow in our team, you will benefit from a safe and respectful research environment, receive dedicated mentorship, and have the freedom to pursue innovative ideas. This scholarship is ideal for researchers eager to develop and use computational tools to study *in silico* cellular responses and who would like to grow in a supportive environment.

**Qualifications**


To qualify as a postdoctoral scholarship holder, the postdoctoral fellow is required to have completed a doctoral degree or a foreign degree deemed equivalent to a doctoral degree. This qualification requirement must be fulfilled no later than at the time of the decision about scholarship recipient.


Priority should be given to candidates who completed their doctoral degree, according to what is stipulated in the paragraph above, no later than three years prior. If there are special reasons, candidates who completed their doctoral degree before that may also be eligible. Special reasons include absence due to illness, parental leave, appointments of trust in trade union organisations, military service, or similar circumstances, as well as clinical practice or other forms of appointment/assignment relevant to the subject area.


The doctoral degree should be in computational/systems biology, computer science, bioinformatics, biostatistics, molecular biology/medicine, or a related field.

**Additional requirements**

* A strong track record in a relevant field of research, documented by first authorships in peer-reviewed original publications
* Comprehensive skills in data analysis and bioinformatics
* Proficiency in programming with Python
* Proficiency in version control (Git, GitHub)


Meriting criteria are:

* Experience in mechanistic and ODE-based computational modeling
* Experience in SBML file handling
* Experience in Docker or other container-based platforms
* Basic understanding of wet-lab work and cell culture


We are looking for candidates with a strong interest in large-scale computational modeling and cancer research. Candidates should be team players, engaging in scientific discussions and exchanging ideas.

**Application**


The application must include:

* A brief description of your research interests and a statement describing why you are interested in the postdoctoral scholarship (max two A4 pages)
* CV (max six A4 pages)
* List of publications (max two A4 pages)
* Degree certificate from doctoral studies and other relevant degrees
* Contact details of two reference persons (email address and phone number with country code)


The application should be written in English or Swedish, and the attached documents should be in Word or PDF format. The application should be registered via Umeå University’s e-recruitment system Varbi and submitted by the deadline 2026-06-15.

**Further information**


For further information about this project, please contact Cemal Erdem at cemal.erdem@umu.se.

**We look forward to receiving your application!**

title: Project Manager – Wearable Airbag Electronics Control Unit Development
description:
Project Manager – Wearable Airbag ECU Development
  
Background
  
Autoliv is driving a new era of rider protection by expanding advanced safety solutions beyond traditional airbags to protect more road users in more scenarios. As part of this mission, we are developing the next‑generation Electronic Control Unit (ECU) to enable smarter, faster, and more scalable safety systems. The ECU project Manager will play a pivotal role in delivering this ECU across a broader product portfolio, accelerating integration into new applications and vehicle platforms. This role directly contributes to Autoliv’s purpose: saving more lives through innovation Link.
  
MISSION
  
Lead and deliver the Next Wearable Airbag Electronics development projects across the full lifecycle, from product evolution and new generations to industrialization and series production.
  
The Project Manager is accountable for planning, cost, quality, risk, and delivery, ensuring effective coordination between system engineering, electronics, embedded software, mechanics, validation, industrialization, suppliers, and operations.
  
The role focuses on the embedded ECU product, its evolution and deployment in the field, while coordinating interfaces with wearables teams and working very closely with mobile application and cloud and crash detection teams.
  
KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  
Project Ownership & Planning
  
  

Own ECU development projects covering product evolutions, new generations, and variants
  
  

Define and control project scope, objectives, milestones, and deliverables
  
  

Build and maintain the global project plan, managing dependencies and critical paths
  
  

Manage project budget and resources (internal and external)
  
  

Anticipate deviations and drive corrective actions
  
  

Technical & Cross‑Functional Coordination
  
  

**Coordinate all technical contributors:**  

System & Electronics Engineering, Embedded Software, Crash detection algorithms, Hardware Design, Mechanical Design, Validation & Test, Industrialization & Manufacturing, Mobile App & cloud.
  
  

Ensure product specifications and system architecture are defined, approved, and respected
  
  

Validate technical trade‑offs in collaboration with the Lead System & Electronics Engineer (cost vs performance, innovation vs risk, short‑term vs long‑term strategy)
  
  

Risk, Safety & Quality Management
  
  

Identify and manage technical, industrial, planning, and supply‑chain risks
  
  

**Ensure safety and quality activities are planned and executed:**
  
  

Functional safety processes, DFMEA / PFMEA, Validation and qualification
  
  

Ensure compliance with internal processes and applicable standards
  
  

Drive escalation and problem resolution on critical issues
  
  

Product Lifecycle & Evolution
  
  

**Manage ECU lifecycle:**  

New generation development, Production support & Continuous improvement
  
  

Coordinate introduction of new features, electronics changes, and architecture evolutions (connectivity, FOTA, security)
  
  

Plan and manage firmware update (FOTA) deployment in coordination with system and software teams
  
  

Suppliers & Industrialization
  
  

Manage relationships with suppliers, manufacturing partners, and external engineering teams
  
  

Support supplier selection and contracting with purchasing
  
  

Ensure supplier deliverables meet technical, planning, and quality expectations
  
  

Coordinate DFM / DFT and ensure readiness for prototypes, pre‑series, and series production
  
  

Monitor manufacturing ramp‑up and ensure proper handover to operations
  
  

Reporting & Communication
  
  

Provide clear, regular project status reporting to management and stakeholders
  
  

Prepare and lead project, milestone, and risk reviews
  
  

Ensure transparent communication and effective decision‑making
  
  

PROFILE
  
Education & Experience
  
  

Engineering degree or equivalent
  
  

5+ years’ experience in project management of embedded or mechatronic systems
  
  

Experience in safety‑critical products is a strong asset
  
  

Having launched a product is a big plus
  
  

Technical Background
  
  

Solid understanding of embedded ECU development, system engineering, and electronics/software constraints
  
  

Familiarity with V‑cycle development, quality and safety processes
  
  

Sufficient technical depth to challenge solutions and drive informed trade‑offs
  
  

Skills & Mindset
  
  

Strong planning, organization, and leadership skills
  
  

Proven experience managing multi‑disciplinary teams and suppliers
  
  

Risk‑ and delivery‑focused, structured, and proactive
  
  

Excellent professional English (written and spoken)
  
  

**Start:** ASAP | Duration: Long term Scope: ECU development projects for wearable airbag systems, from evolution to next‑generation platforms

title: Lead System & Electronics Engineer – Wearable Airbag ECU
description:
MISSION
  
Own the system and electronics engineering of a wearable airbag vest ECU, from architecture definition through industrialization and manufacturing release.
  
The role combines system engineering ownership and electronics design leadership for an existing product platform that will evolve across multiple generations, with increased functionality, connectivity, and performance.
  
The objective is to deliver safe, robust, manufacturable, and evolvable ECU solutions that meet functional, safety, cost, quality, and planning requirements for a critical safety application.
  
The role acts as the technical reference, working closely with the Project Manager, hardware and software teams, industrialization, suppliers, and validation teams. In addition, the System lead will be the main technical contact for the Product teams and will work on the integration with them. The team is located in France & Sweden mainly. Travel to France, China, US & other countries is expected regularly (Several times a year)
  
KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  
System & Product Architecture
  
  

Define, maintain, and evolve the ECU system architecture across generations (sensing, processing, power, safety interfaces, connectivity, updates)
  
  

Perform functional analysis including safety and cybersecurity
  
  

Define system modularity, interfaces, constraints, and services,
  
  

Ensure architectural consistency across hardware, embedded software, and connectivity (mobile gateway & cloud interfaces)
  
  

Own the technical roadmap for future product generations
  
  

Electronics Design Leadership
  
  

Lead and approve key electronics design choices (MCU/SoC, sensors, power architecture, connectivity modules, safety‑critical circuits)
  
  

Identify technical risks and design hard points and define mitigation actions
  
  

Validate design results with hardware and software leads
  
  

Ensure compliance with cost, power consumption, robustness, and environmental constraints specific to wearable products
  
  

Development Process, Safety & Compliance
  
  

Apply suitable systems engineering methods, e.g., following the V‑cycle
  
  

Ensure alignment with functional safety and quality processes
  
  

Lead or contribute to risk and safety analyses (DFMEA, PFMEA, hazard analysis)
  
  

Ensure full traceability from requirements to verification and validation
  
  

Requirements & Documentation
  
  

Define and approve system requirements, product specifications, and interface specifications
  
  

Ensure consistency of system documentation (item definition, architecture, interfaces)
  
  

Manage change impact analysis across variants and generations
  
  

Maintain the design definition file for prototype and series production
  
  

Connectivity, FOTA & Lifecycle (ECU Scope)
  
  

Define ECU‑side architecture for secure connectivity and cloud interaction
  
  

Own the FOTA strategy (architecture, safety, rollback, validation)
  
  

Ensure long‑term maintainability and field robustness (Mobile app and cloud development are out of scope; ECU interfaces are in scope.)
  
  

Prototyping & Industrialization
  
  

Define prototype needs and lead technical validation
  
  

Support or lead Design for Manufacturing and Test (DFM/DFT)
  
  

Define system‑level test and production test strategies
  
  

Ensure readiness for industrialization and manufacturing release
  
  

Technical Leadership
  
  

Coordinate the system and electronics design team
  
  

Organize technical and architecture reviews
  
  

Interface with project management, development teams, manufacturing, and suppliers
  
  

Provide technical support and training when required
  
  

PROFILE
  
Education & Experience
  
  

Engineering degree (Electronics, Embedded Systems, Mechatronics or equivalent)
  
  

4–6 years’ experience in embedded real‑time systems
  
  

Proven background in system architecture, electronics leadership, and safety‑critical products
  
  

Skills
  
  

Strong expertise in embedded electronics and ECU design
  
  

Solid knowledge of embedded development, low‑power systems, functional safety, connectivity, and FOTA
  
  

Experience with prototyping and industrialization
  
  

Soft Skills
  
  

Strong technical leadership and communication
  
  

Autonomous, structured, and solution‑oriented
  
  

Excellent professional English
  
  

**Start:** ASAP | Duration: Long term Scope: System & Electronics ownership for current and next‑generation wearable airbag ECUs

title: Data Analyst Engineer
description:
A Data Analyst is responsible for transforming raw data into meaningful insights that support strategic decision‑making across the organization. This role involves collecting, cleaning, analyzing, and visualizing data from multiple sources while collaborating with cross‑functional teams to improve business performance, operational efficiency, and data‑driven outcomes.
  
Key Responsibilities
  
Analyze large, complex datasets using SQL, Python, and BI tools to identify trends, patterns, and actionable insights.
  
Develop interactive dashboards and reports using Power BI, Tableau, or similar visualization platforms to support business stakeholders.
  
Build and maintain ETL pipelines to ensure accurate, timely, and reliable data flow across systems.
  
Partner with product, engineering, finance, and operations teams to define data requirements, KPIs, and reporting standards.
  
Conduct statistical analyses, including A/B testing, forecasting, and predictive modeling, to support business experiments and strategic planning.
  
Ensure data quality, governance, and compliance with regulatory standards such as HIPAA, GDPR, or industry‑specific frameworks.
  
Document data processes, business logic, and analytical methodologies to support transparency and reproducibility.
  
Support automation initiatives by optimizing workflows, reducing manual reporting, and improving data accessibility.
  
  

Required Skills & Qualifications
  
Strong proficiency in SQL, Python, and data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau).
  
Experience with ETL tools and cloud data platforms such as Snowflake, AWS, Azure, or Databricks.
  
Solid understanding of statistical methods, hypothesis testing, and predictive analytics.
  
Ability to translate business questions into analytical solutions with clear, data‑driven recommendations.
  
Excellent communication skills with the ability to present insights to technical and non‑technical audiences.
  
Knowledge of data governance, security, and compliance standards.
  
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Engineering, Statistics, or a related field.
  
  

Preferred Qualifications
  
Experience with workflow orchestration tools (Airflow, ADF, Jenkins).
  
Familiarity with APIs, automation, and LLM‑based workflows.
  
Certifications in cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP).
  
Background in healthcare, finance, supply chain, or other regulated industries.

title: Product Operations Specialist
description:
**About Stayntouch**  

Stayntouch is redefining how hotels operate. Headquartered in Bethesda, MD, we deliver a cloud-native PMS built for modern hospitality - flexible, intuitive, mobile-first, and designed to empower teams from the lobby to the back office. Our platform streamlines operations, unlocks new revenue opportunities, and elevates every step of the guest journey. Hoteliers can run their entire operation from any device, enabling staff to break free from the front desk and guests to personalize their stay from check-in to check-out. Trusted by iconic properties and brands, Stayntouch is the PMS for hotels that demand both simplicity and power.

**The Role**
------------


Stayntouch is looking for a Product Operations Specialist to help improve how product work moves from research through delivery and into go-to-market.

  

This is a hands-on, cross-functional role focused on helping Product Managers and Product Owners bring features successfully to launch and ensure smooth alignment across Engineering, GTM, and the wider business. As Product Owners move onto new priorities, this role helps maintain continuity and clarity across delivery and release activities.


This role works closely with the SVP of Product and Director of Product to improve how we operate as a product organization.

**Key Responsibilities**
------------------------

* Improve how product work flows across research, delivery, and go-to-market
* Ensure product features are clearly prepared, communicated, and successfully launched across Engineering and GTM
* Improve handoffs between Product, Engineering, Sales, and Customer-facing teams
* Drive consistency in planning, communication, and product delivery practices
* Improve visibility of roadmap, delivery progress, and product outcomes
* Help implement practical improvements to product processes in alignment with the Product team

**Qualifications**
------------------

* 2–4 years’ experience in Product, Product Operations, or a similar role
* Hospitality / hospitality tech experience is required
* Strong understanding of how product teams work end-to-end (research delivery launch adoption)
* Comfortable working across teams and driving alignment
* Structured, pragmatic, and hands-on

### **Why this role**


This role ensures Product Managers and Product Owners are supported in successfully bringing work to market, while improving how Product, Engineering, and GTM operate together.

**Why Join Stayntouch?**
------------------------

* Help shape the future of a category-leading hospitality technology company
* Work with innovative hotel brands and management groups around the world
* Own and scale the full marketing engine with executive visibility and impact
* Join a collaborative, high-growth environment where your work directly influences business outcomes
* Competitive compensation, benefits, and flexible work environment

title: Produktionsingenjör
description:
* Permanent
* Experienced Professionals
* Products & Systems Engineering
* ID 475257-en\_US

At Capgemini Engineering, the world leader in engineering services, we bring together a global team of engineers, scientists, and architects to help the world’s most innovative companies unleash their potential. From autonomous cars to life-saving robots, our digital and software technology experts think outside the box as they provide unique R&D and engineering services across all industries. Join us for a career full of opportunities. Where you can make a difference. Where no two days are the same.


Din roll  

Capgemini Engineering Västerås söker nu en produktionsingenjör med teknisk utbildning och analytisk förmåga för att arbeta med produktionstider och kapacitet. Huvudsyftet är att skapa ett faktabaserat underlag för att förbättra och utveckla produktionsflöden genom tidsstudier, dataanalys och kapacitetsberäkningar.  

  

*I den här rollen spelar du en nyckelroll i:*  

* Genomföra tidsstudier i produktionen för att samla in data.
* Dokumentera olika typer av tider som cykeltider, ställtider, maskintider och väntetider.
* Sammanställa och analysera den insamlade tidsdatan.
* Utföra kapacitetsberäkningar för olika delar av produktionen.
* Identifiera samband mellan produktionsbeläggning, flöde och kapacitet.
* Presentera resultat och slutsatser på ett tydligt och begripligt sätt.

  

Din profil  

* Teknisk utbildning inom exempelvis produktionsteknik eller industriell ekonomi.
* Grundläggande förståelse för produktionsprocesser och flöden.
* God analytisk förmåga och ett starkt intresse för datahantering.
* Vana att arbeta strukturerat med verktyg som Excel.
* Förmåga att arbeta självständigt men också i team.
* Noggrannhet, nyfikenhet och en vilja att arbeta ute i produktionen.

  

Vad vi tror kommer få dig att trivas  

Innovativ miljö: Hos oss får du chansen att jobba med den senaste tekniken och vara en del av ett dynamiskt team som älskar att skapa värde genom innovation.  

Karriärutveckling: Vi bryr oss om din framtid och erbjuder utvecklingsprogram och certifieringar i de senaste teknologierna, så att du kan växa och bli den bästa versionen av dig själv.  

Flexibelt arbete: Vi förstår att livet är mer än bara jobb. Därför erbjuder vi möjligheter till distansarbete och flexibla arbetstider för att du ska må bra och trivas.  

Teamkänsla: Vi är som en stor familj med en härlig teamkänsla. Genom att leva efter våra värdeord som mod, passion, laganda och entreprenörskap - skapar vi en arbetsmiljö där alla kan trivas.  

  

Ansökan  

Som en del av vår rekryteringsprocess kan identitetsverifiering och bakgrundskontroller komma att genomföras vid behov.

title: Account Executive
description:
Darktrace is a global leader in AI for cybersecurity that keeps organizations ahead of the changing threat landscape every day. Founded in 2013, Darktrace provides the essential cybersecurity platform protecting nearly 10,000 organizations from unknown threats using its proprietary AI.


The Darktrace Active AI Security Platform™ delivers a proactive approach to cyber resilience to secure the business across the entire digital estate – from network to cloud to email. Breakthrough innovations from our R&D teams have resulted in over 200 patent applications filed. Darktrace’s platform and services are supported by over 2,400 employees around the world. To learn more, visit http://www.darktrace.com .

  

**Job D** **escription** **:**

Darktrace offers outstanding growth potential for your career along with uncapped commission in the emerging sector of cyber security, using artificial intelligence and machine learning. This is a full-cycle sales role and you will be responsible for hunting new logos, expanding market share, and maximising revenue growth in the Nordic region therefore fluency in Swedish or Norwegian is essential. This role requires a self-starter with strong sales acumen, capable of positioning Darktrace solutions to C-level executives and security professionals. You will own the full sales cycle, from prospecting to closing, while also identifying upsell opportunities within your territory to maximize customer lifetime value.


This role is remote if based in Stockholm with access to a shared workspace or hybrid with minimum 3 days a week in the office if based in London.


**What will I be doing:**

You will own the full sales cycle for enterprise-level accounts, encompassing the entire 360 sales process. This includes establishing and working with partners in your territory and presenting and demonstrating Darktrace's cyber threat defense solutions to CISOs and information security experts. You will also attend marketing events and security conferences, and close the largest deals in the business with the support of a dedicated SDR team for lead generation. Collaborating with in-house technical experts, you will present and demonstrate our cutting-edge threat defense solutions to key stakeholders.


Darktrace provides an excellent onboarding experience, extensive training, and ongoing support from mentors.


**What experience do I need:**

We are seeking ambitious and driven individuals with previous B2B sales experience to join our ever growing friendly and social team of Account Executives covering the Nordics Market. As an Account Executive you'll play a key role in driving the sales operation for the Nordic region.


You'll be currently selling at an enterprise level in a closing role and you are a confident, polished communicator capable of building strong and lasting working relationships. You are motivated by generous uncapped commission and thrive in a target-driven environment, you will also:


* Be fluent Swedish or Norwegian (spoken and written)
* Have an interest in cybersecurity and being tech-savvy,
* Have experience in a high-growth business environment or start-up.
* 2+ years of experience in a full‑cycle 360 sales role, owning the entire sales process from prospecting to close
* Proven experience working in a partner‑led or channel sales model, including Resellers, MSSPs or distributors
* Background in SaaS, cybersecurity, IT infrastructure, or enterprise software is strongly preferred

  

**Benefits:**

* 25 days’ holiday + all national public holidays,
* Additional day off for your birthday,
* Group Life & Accident insurance,
* Employer-provided pension scheme,
* Medical insurance,
* Long term disability insurance,
* Employee Assistance Program.

title: Systems Engineer (Compute & Storage)
description:
### **Join Telavox as a Systems Engineer for Compute & Storage ️**


Are you a hands-on systems engineer who thrives in operational depth and wants to own the infrastructure that the rest of engineering runs on? At Telavox, we run our own infrastructure. On-prem Kubernetes, our own Ceph clusters, and a fleet of physical servers across six countries. We are providers of infrastructure, not consumers of cloud.


Telavox is a fast-growing digital telco that combines the reliability of telecom with the agility of SaaS across Europe and beyond. As our platform grows in usage and geographic footprint, we need someone who can carry real day-to-day operational ownership while helping us level up how we work.


We are looking for a Systems Engineer to join our Infrastructure Engineering team. On-prem is a deliberate choice. It is what our product and economics demand, and we are investing in operating it well.

#### **About the job**


This role spans both the operational and the architectural. Some days, you are deep in a cluster issue or pushing through an upgrade. Other days, you are designing how things should work at scale and building the processes that make the team more effective. Both matter equally here. We are not expecting you to master every corner of our stack from day one - if you bring real depth in one or two areas, the team has you covered on the rest.

**Your day-to-day work includes:**

* Keep clusters healthy, respond to issues, and help with capacity and upgrades across our compute and storage infrastructure.
* Design how things should work over time. Upgrade procedures across regions, runbooks for recurring operations, and automation for what is currently manual.
* Carry that work out in practice. Automation is the direction, but some manual work will always exist for the not-yet-automated, or what cannot reasonably be automated. You would be comfortable with both ends.
* Contribute to architecture and tooling decisions in the areas you are strong in, and learn from teammates in the areas you are not.

**The three areas you would work across:**

* Kubernetes on-prem is the runtime for most of our application workloads. Cluster lifecycle management - upgrades, capacity, and version drift across regions - is a real area we want to invest in.
* Ceph (block and S3) is gaining traction across our regions, with Sweden running the largest footprint. The core challenge is getting the operational foundations right before adoption forces the issue. Stability, security, and predictable scaling need to be solved at 1x, not 10x.
* Server and OS fleet management spans six countries. Most sites are small; Sweden is the largest. We use Ansible-driven configuration and want to keep tightening the lifecycle - upgrade procedures, patching cadence, and drift control.

**How we work together:**


You'd join Infrastructure Engineering, around 15 people across two groups (Network and Developer Platform).


* The role is in Malmö at Gjuteriet, our new vibrant HQ. Sites in other countries are managed remotely — occasional travel is possible but not expected.
* No mandatory on-call rotation. There's a shared org-wide on-call line, and Compute and Storage have an informal secondary list, called maybe 1-3 times a month total per domain.
* We use Claude Code across engineering, including infrastructure. You'd join the team in figuring out where and how it can be safely and usefully applied to platform work.

#### **About you**


You are an autonomous engineer who is comfortable taking an unclear problem, scoping it, and seeing it through. You see automation as the direction, even when today's task is manual. Repetition is a trigger for automation, and risk is something you factor in from the start, not discover at the end.


You are pragmatic enough to roll up your sleeves when the automation is not there yet, knowing that some tasks will always require a human touch.

**We are looking for someone with:**

* Strong Linux fundamentals are a must-have; it is the substrate everything else sits on
* Operational depth in at least one of: on-prem Kubernetes operations, Ceph or comparable distributed storage, or server and OS fleet lifecycle management
* Comfortable owning work autonomously, taking an unclear problem, scoping it, and seeing it through

**Strongly valued:**

* A second area from the list above
* Experience with Ansible or comparable configuration management
* Track record of operating systems at scale across geographically distributed sites
* Comfort with sub-OS work (firmware, BIOS, BMC/IPMI) is a nice addition, since we do some of that ourselves


It´s a mid-to-senior level role, with a path toward Senior and eventually Domain Owner in Compute or Storage as you build context.

#### **Join us at Telavox**


Since Telavox's journey started in 2002, we have strengthened our position as the leading forward-thinking communication platform for businesses. As a digital mobile operator, we develop and own our all-in-one communication platform, which integrates telephony, PBX, messaging, meetings, and contact centers, giving businesses a smarter way to connect.  

  

Today, we’re a thriving company with 1.9 billion SEK in revenue and 500+ Telavoxers across nine countries. We embrace AI and automation to push the boundaries of business communication.  

  

We offer flexible work options and adaptable hours, giving you the freedom to balance life and career while staying connected to our vibrant Telavox culture.


Read more about our new HQ in Malmö !

### **How to apply!**


At Telavox, we don't focus on fitting in; we focus on making room for everyone.  

  

We’re always on the lookout for great talent, so applications are reviewed continuously. If you're ready to be part of an innovative, AI-driven telecom company, apply today with your CV and cover letter in English.


For any questions, feel free to reach out to Linus Ahlberg, Engineering Manager at linus.ahlberg@telavox.se, or Talent Acquisition Partner Ami Faraguna at ann-marie.faraguna@telavox.com

 **Learn more about what we do! ️**

title: Senior Systems Engineer (Domain Owner)
description:
**Join Telavox as Senior Systems Engineer (Domain Owner)**
----------------------------------------------------------

Are you a senior engineer who wants real ownership of the platform you build? At Telavox, the Delivery domain - CI/CD, artifact management, secrets, and the infrastructure self-service tooling - is critical infrastructure for our entire engineering organisation. We're hiring someone to own it.


Telavox is a fast-growing digital telco that combines the reliability of telecom with the agility of SaaS across Europe and beyond. As our platform scales, the experience our developers have when shipping code matters more than ever. We want someone to make that experience genuinely great.


We are looking for a Systems Engineer for Delivery to join our Infrastructure Engineering team. This is a hands-on senior role where you set technical direction, build the roadmap, and contribute substantially to the technical work itself.

### **About the job**


As a Systems Engineer for Delivery, you are the person who can answer "what are the most important things in Delivery right now, and why?" better than anyone else. You own the roadmap, align priorities across teams, and contribute substantially to architecture and implementation.


Being a Domain Owner is a stewardship role, not a management one. You wouldn't be staffing the team or making people decisions - you'd be setting technical direction, communicating it, and driving the work. Today, Delivery is touched by several engineers as a slice of their time, and we want to bring focus and coherence to it.

**Your day-to-day work includes:**

* Set technical direction for Delivery: define quality standards, prioritise investments, and maintain a clear roadmap (Jira, RICE scoring). Align priorities via the bi-weekly Domain Owner Forum.
* Partner with ~10 engineering teams across product areas to understand needs and translate them into platform improvements, and treat the platform as a product.
* Improve CI/CD (Jenkins), focusing on stability, observability, and scalability; reduce noise from flaky vs real failures.
* Evolve the GitOps-based deployment setup through continuous improvement.
* Define and own SLOs for the Delivery experience from the ground up.
* Evaluate the need for an Internal Developer Portal and guide the decision.
* Support the evolution of Delivery for AI-augmented development workflows.


There's also an adjacent room in Observability if it interests you. We're consolidating onto an LGTM-focused stack.

**How we work together:**

* You'd join Infrastructure Engineering, around 15 people across two groups (Network and Developer Platform).
* The role is in Malmö at Gjuteriet, our new vibrant HQ.
* No mandatory on-call rotation. There's a shared org-wide on-call line, and Delivery has an informal secondary list called maybe 1-3 times a month total.
* We use Claude Code across engineering. The software-dev side is well into it; the platform side is leaning in carefully, and you'd help shape that for Delivery.

### **About you**


You are a senior engineer who is self-driving, product-minded, and comfortable owning ambiguity. You have worked with CI/CD systems at scale, know their failure modes, and have engaged directly with developers' pain points. You can turn fuzzy organisational friction into a clear technical roadmap, and you see things through.


You believe great internal platforms are built by people who genuinely understand what developers need, and you are the kind of engineer who goes and finds out.

**We are looking for someone with:**

* Strong Kubernetes and cloud-native delivery experience (GitOps, ArgoCD or equivalent)
* Hands-on familiarity with CI/CD systems at scale and their failure modes
* Demonstrated product-mindedness: you've engaged directly with developer or end-user needs
* Self-driving in scoping and prioritising your domain. You can turn fuzzy friction into clear roadmaps and follow through

**Strongly valued:**

* Observability stack experience (metrics, logs, traces). LGTM stack a plus
* Experience building or owning an Internal Developer Portal or platform service catalog
* Working knowledge of how AI/LLM tooling fits into developer workflows, or strong opinions about how it should be done


This is a Senior role with explicit domain stewardship responsibilities. Paths from here include growing into a Staff IC scope or, if it interests you, moving into management.

#### **Join us at Telavox**


Since Telavox's journey started in 2002, we have strengthened our position as the leading forward-thinking communication platform for businesses. As a digital mobile operator, we develop and own our all-in-one communication platform, which integrates telephony, PBX, messaging, meetings, and contact centers, giving businesses a smarter way to connect.  

  

Today, we’re a thriving company with 1.9 billion SEK in revenue and 500+ Telavoxers across nine countries. We embrace AI and automation to push the boundaries of business communication.  

  

We offer flexible work options and adaptable hours, giving you the freedom to balance life and career while staying connected to our vibrant Telavox culture.


Read more about our new HQ in Malmö !

### **How to apply!**


At Telavox, we don't focus on fitting in; we focus on making room for everyone.  

  

We’re always on the lookout for great talent, so applications are reviewed continuously. If you're ready to be part of an innovative, AI-driven telecom company, apply today with your CV and cover letter in English.


For any questions, feel free to reach out to Linus Ahlberg, Engineering Manager at linus.ahlberg@telavox.se or Talent Acquisition Partner Ami Faraguna at ann-marie.faraguna@telavox.com

 **Learn more about what we do! ️**

title: Senior M365 Security Engineer
description:
**Do you want to be at the forefront of protecting our society against cyber threats? We are looking for a Senior M365 Security Engineer to team up with the most dedicated team of cyber specialists in our Secure Infrastructure domain.**

##### **About Truesec – A Leading Cybersecurity Company**


Since 2005, Truesec has been a native cybersecurity company, driven by one single purpose: Creating safety and sustainability in a digital world by preventing cyber breach and minimizing impact.


Over the years, we’ve earned the trust of organizations worldwide and gained a strong reputation internationally. Our team consists of dedicated specialists covering the entire spectrum of cybersecurity – with capabilities in Predict, Prevent, Protect, Respond, and Recover.


Today, we employ around 350 employees – and as cybercrime grows, we need to grow. To support that goal, we are now looking for a Senior M365 Security Engineer who wants to make a difference in our Secure Infrastructure domain.

##### **Secure Infrastructure**


Looking at the actual threat landscape today, it’s more important than ever to design and build infrastructure solutions that incorporate correct and relevant security features. The team works closely together using their combined knowledge and experience gathered from different projects and Incident Response cases to help our customers stay ahead of the threat actors. We continuously help customers improve their security posture within cloud, data center, endpoint management, identity, M365, and networking.

##### **The Role**


Truesec is seeking a Senior Microsoft 365 engineer for a full-time role in Stockholm or remote. The cloud engineer interfaces regularly with team members and customers, determines technical requirements, feasibility of projects and expectations. You are responsible for maintaining, implementing, and designing client-facing solutions primarily in M365 Security and Entra ID. This role is for someone who wants to be hands-on in a technical role leaning heavily towards Cloud and Cloud Security. You must be able to think on your feet and you’ve got to want to learn and be part of a highly skilled and innovative team. Other responsibilities you might have can be:

* Run health and security checks within M365 and Entra ID
* Provide customers recommendations and prioritization of how to improve their security posture over time.
* Implement solutions based on Statement of Work with customers

##### **Your Potential Background**


We believe that your background probably entails 5+ years of experience and expertise within the Microsoft public cloud, such as M365 Security, Intune and Entra ID, including troubleshooting, deploying, and configuring of said solutions. It's likely that you also have experience with hybrid environments, Conditional Access, and Azure Security.

**Required Skills:**

* Strong Experience of M365 Security platform
* Strong Experience of Entra ID
* Genuine interest of helping customers within Cybersecurity
* Ability to prioritize and create implementation plans for Customers

**Bonus Skills:**

* Experience within Client Management
* Understanding of M365 licensing
* Ability to script and automate
* Experience with hybrid environments

*Due to the sensitivity in our assignments, we require Swedish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish work permit.*

##### **Join Our Mission Toward a Safe Digital Future**


If you truly want to make a difference and make our world a little bit safer, this is the place for you. At Truesec, you'll get to be at the forefront of fighting cybercrime instead of reading about it in the news. This is your chance to be part of an exciting journey in a leading cybersecurity company - and learn from some of the best in their field.


So, are you ready to team up with the most dedicated team of experts and be part of a meaningful mission? Then we are excited to get to know you!

title: iOS Engineer – UI / Application Layer
description:
**The app layer is where great user experiences come to life. In this role, you’ll work close to the interface, the application logic, and the connection between client apps and background services. It’s a great fit if you enjoy building polished, intuitive iOS experiences while still understanding how the systems behind them work together.**

#### **What you'll do**

Build and refine user-facing features in iOS applications, with focus on the application layer, UI, and interaction design.


Work with SwiftUI, UIKit, Swift, or Objective-C depending on the codebase and product needs.


Translate product and design requirements into clean, maintainable, and testable iOS components.


Collaborate with designers, backend engineers, and other app developers to create smooth and reliable user experiences.


Ensure that UI components interact effectively with background services, data flows, and asynchronous processes.


Improve performance, accessibility, and usability across key user journeys.


Debug, refactor, and modernize existing code while balancing quality, delivery, and practical trade-offs.


Contribute to technical discussions, code reviews, and continuous improvement of the iOS application architecture.

#### **What we’re looking for**

**Problem-solving clarity** - Ability to explain your thinking, ask clarifying questions, and iterate from simple to optimized


**Technical fundamentals** - Can reason about complexity, understand practical trade-offs, and write clean code


**Communication** - Easy to talk to, take feedback without defensiveness, and discuss assumptions explicitly


**Genuine enthusiasm** - Show real interest in hard problems, not just getting a job


**Learning agility** - Willing to pick up new technology, work with legacy code, and adapt quickly

#### **Must have**

**Computer science fundamentals**, including algorithms, data structures, and architecture. Ideally B.Sc. or M.Sc. in Computer Science.

**5+ years of professional iOS development experience**, including strong hands-on work with **SwiftUI, UIKit, Objective-C, or Swift****.**


Understanding of i**OS architecture, data flow, and component structure**.


Ability to write **clean, testable, and maintainable code**.


Basic understanding of **asynchronous and concurrent programming**.

**Strong communication skills** and enthusiasm for **mobile platforms and user-focused app development.**


Passion for **design and user experience.**

#### **Nice to have**

Experience with **SwiftUI** and **modern UI patterns**, such as MVVM, Combine, etc.


Knowledge of **animation**, **accessibility**, **and performance optimization**.


Understanding of interaction between **UI and background processes**.


Experience with **build and test automation**, such as CI/CD, Xcode Cloud.

**Join a product-focused engineering environment where thoughtful design, clean implementation, and smooth user experiences matter. You’ll work close to the app layer, building iOS interfaces and application flows that feel intuitive, reliable, and well connected to the systems behind them.**

title: Senior Machine Learning / Computer Vision Engineer
description:
We are looking for a Senior Machine Learning engineer with experience in Computer Vision to join our team. You’ll play an important role in designing the AI solutions that power the core of our technology, while also owning an important part of the ML stack.

 **Key Qualifications:**

* **Experience:** 5+ years of proven expertise in **Computer Vision** and **Machine Learning** development.
* **System Architecture:** Demonstrated ability to design and scale production ML architectures and automated pipelines (**MLOps**, **CI/CD**, **Docker**, and **Cloud**).
* **Deep Learning:** Expert-level proficiency in **PyTorch** or **TensorFlow**, with specialized experience in model optimization (**Quantization**, **Pruning**) for resource-constrained **embedded devices**.
* **High-Performance Engineering:** Mastery of clean, modular **Python** and proficiency in **C++** for performance-critical applications and deployment.
* **Leadership & Impact:** A track record of leading complex ML projects from conceptualization to full-scale production deployment.
* Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to work in a collaborative team environment.
* Excellent communication skills in English.
* Experience in management of R&D projects and handling customer requests from development to production level and following the expected timeline.

**Responsibilities:**

* Design AI solutions for Computer Vision applications, with a focus on latency and low-computational power systems.
* Design and maintain MLOps pipelines to manage experiments, guaranteeing their reproducibility.
* Taking a part in designing and running efficient large data pipelines that feed into our training infrastructure.
* Stay updated on the latest advancements in machine learning and apply them to our technology.

title: Postdoctoral Scholarship (2 years) within computational modeling of human cell signaling and metabolism
description:
Umeå University is one of Sweden’s largest higher education institutions with over 37,000 students and about 4,700 employees. The University offers a diversity of high-quality education and world-leading research in several fields. Notably, the groundbreaking discovery of the CRISPR-Cas9 gene-editing tool, which was awarded the Nobel Prize in Chemistry, was made here. At Umeå University, everything is close. Our cohesive campuses make it easy to meet, work together and exchange knowledge, which promotes a dynamic and open culture. The ongoing societal transformation and large green investments in northern Sweden create enormous opportunities and complex challenges. For Umeå University, conducting research about – and in the middle of – a society in transition is key. We also take pride in delivering education to enable regions to expand quickly and sustainably. In fact, the future is made here. Are you interested in learning more? Read here


The Faculty of Medicine, which consists of 12 departments, is responsible for biomedical research and courses in the field of nursing and health care and has an extensive research and graduate education in more than 80 subjects.

**Postdoctoral** **Scholarship (2 years) within computational modeling of human cell signaling and metabolism**


The *Department of Medical Biosciences* is offering a postdoctoral scholarship within the project “Unified computational modeling of human cell signaling and metabolism to advance precision oncology”. The scholarship is full-time for 2 years, with access starting in September 2026 or by agreement.

**Departmental specific information**


The research will be carried out in the laboratory of Cemal Erdem at the Department of Medical Biosciences, which offers an international, collaborative, and open-minded research environment. Please visit the lab’s webpage for more information: https://erdemlab.github.io.


The Erdem research group is part of the SciLifeLab and Wallenberg National Program for Data-Driven Life Science (DDLS), which aims to recruit and train the next-generation of data-driven life scientists and to create globally leading computational and data science capabilities in Sweden. The program is funded with a total of 3.3 billion SEK over 12 years from the Knut and Alice Wallenberg (KAW) Foundation. DDLS uses data, computational methods and artificial intelligence to study biological systems and processes at all levels, from molecular structures and cellular processes to human health and global ecosystems.

**Project description**


A key missing capability in current cancer research is the ability to predict how a particular cancer cell will respond to a perturbation (e.g., drug inhibition or gene knockdown). However, building computational models of complex, large-scale, and incompletely understood systems remains extremely challenging. To address this issue, we recently developed the SPARCED pipeline to convert structured lists of species, parameters, and reaction types into an SBML (Systems Biology Markup Language) model file, and we created a model based on one of the largest pan-cancer signaling models in the literature. SPARCED is compatible with high-performance and cloud computing, can simulate thousands to millions of single-cell trajectories, is easily expandable with new pathways, and is retrainable with new omics data for new cellular contexts.


SPARCED currently has two interlinked modules, where the first part simulates protein signaling cascades and the second part simulates mRNA levels using stochastic methods. Doing so enables SPARCED to realistically represent mRNA numbers (or bursts), which are slightly different for each simulation, and thus a single-cell like behavior. However, SPARCED currently does not contain one key aspect: cellular metabolism. Recently, a metabolic atlas of human cells has been released, making it now possible to merge protein signaling models with metabolic network information.


In this project, the postdoctoral fellow will have the opportunity to:

* Curate the human metabolic atlas information into SPARCED format and incorporate them to generate SPARCED-Metabolome (SPARCED-Me) model.
* Curate other databases to incorporate required crosstalk interactions to construct a complete model.
* Help collect multi-omics (transcriptomic, proteomic, and metabolomic) data from different cancer cell lines to parametrize the SPARCED-Me model in various contexts.


In our lab, we are committed to fostering a collaborative and inclusive environment that promotes reproducible and rigorous research practices. As a postdoctoral fellow in our team, you will benefit from a safe and respectful research environment, receive dedicated mentorship, and have the freedom to pursue innovative ideas. This scholarship is ideal for researchers eager to develop and use computational tools to study *in silico* cellular processes and who would like to grow in a supportive environment.


This postdoctoral scholarship is financed by the Kempe Foundation and administered by Umeå University.

**Qualifications**


To qualify as a postdoctoral scholarship holder, the postdoctoral fellow is required to have completed a doctoral degree or a foreign degree deemed equivalent to a doctoral degree. This qualification requirement must be fulfilled no later than at the time of the decision about scholarship recipient.


Priority should be given to candidates who completed their doctoral degree, according to what is stipulated in the paragraph above, no later than three years prior. If there are special reasons, candidates who completed their doctoral degree before that may also be eligible. Special reasons include absence due to illness, parental leave, appointments of trust in trade union organisations, military service, or similar circumstances, as well as clinical practice or other forms of appointment/assignment relevant to the subject area.


The doctoral degree should be in computational/systems biology, computer science, bioinformatics, biostatistics, molecular biology/medicine, or a related field.

**Additional requirements**

A strong track record in a relevant field of research, documented by first authorships in peer-reviewed original publications Comprehensive skills in data analysis and bioinformatics Proficiency in programming with Python Proficiency in version control (Git, GitHub)


Meriting criteria are:

* Experience in metabolic network modeling
* Experience in metabolomic data analysis and research
* Experience in ODE-based computational modeling
* Experience in SBML file handling
* Experience in Docker or other container-based platforms
* Basic understanding of wet-lab work and cell culture


We are looking for candidates with a strong interest in large-scale computational modeling and cancer research. Candidates should be team players, engaging in scientific discussions and exchanging ideas.

**Application**


The application must include:

* A brief description of your research interests and a statement describing why you are interested in the postdoctoral scholarship (max two A4 pages)
* CV (max six A4 pages)
* List of publications (max two A4 pages)
* Degree certificate from doctoral studies and other relevant degrees
* Contact details of two reference persons (email address and phone number with country code)


The application should be written in English or Swedish, and the attached documents should be in Word or PDF format. The application should be registered via Umeå University’s e-recruitment system Varbi and submitted by the deadline 2026-06-15.

**Further information**


For further information about this project, please contact Cemal Erdem at cemal.erdem@umu.se.

**We look forward to receiving your application!**

title: Senior Field Marketing Manager
description:
**What makes us Qlik?**

A Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ Leader for 15 years in a row, Qlik transforms complex data landscapes into actionable insights, driving strategic business outcomes. Serving over 40,000 global customers, our portfolio leverages pervasive data quality and advanced AI/ML capabilities that lead to better decisions, faster.

We excel in integration and governance solutions that work with diverse data sources, and our real-time analytics uncover hidden patterns, empowering teams to address complex challenges and seize new opportunities.
**The Senior Field Marketing Nordics Role**

We are seeking an experienced Senior Field Marketing Manager to own and execute the field marketing strategy in the Nordics region, with the objective to deepen account engagement, put our sellers in front of the right audience and ultimately help drive sales pipeline.
**What makes this role interesting?**
This is an opportunity for someone to consolidate their past marketing experience and embrace an opportunity for personal and professional growth. The ideal candidate will be tenacious, an experienced business operator and comfortable with navigating change. They should also be tech savvy with deep experience of marketing technology solutions and confident in using AI within daily marketing workflows. In return we offer a dynamic, fast-paced working environment with many opportunities to learn and grow, working with the best team in the Nordics!
**Here’s how you’ll be making an impact:*** As a Senior Field Marketing Manager, you’re responsible for partnering with the local Sales leadership team to translate business objectives into an efficient and effective integrated marketing plan aligned with GTM (direct & indirect business)
* You’ll act as a strategic partner and collaborate with partners / BDR / Sales and cross -functional marketing teams to identify gaps and opportunities to build Qlik brand equity (full portfolio) as well as drive pipeline and revenue growth
* You’ll oversee the extension of global marketing activities into EMEA North/Nordics market providing opportunities to connect and further engage with prospects and customers in the region.
* You’ll execute a variety of marketing programs and activities including cross sell/upsell campaigns, acquisition campaigns, and events/webinars. You’ll leverage our Tech Alliances and Global System Integrators partners to gain new audiences and expand our reach
* You’ll support our focus partners (re-sellers) to generate revenue by managing MDF budget and set up co-marketing efforts aligned to specific GTMs
* You’ll measure marketing impact and evaluate event/campaign performance in order to drive continuous improvement and improve the overall effectiveness of marketing
* You will be the voice of Field Marketing in Nordics, advocating for the needs of the region and providing direction into global marketing functions to influence centralized plans and execution


**We’re looking for a teammate with:**
* Deep understanding of how to build, deliver and optimize marketing plans, with a focus on driving results and business impact
* Data driven marketer who is digital savvy and understands today’s marketing landscape
* Ability to work effectively in a distributed team / matrixed environment – proven ability to collaborate with multiple stakeholders to drive a project or program to success
* Excellent verbal and written communications, with the proven ability to develop relationships and communicate with all levels of the organization
* Strong analytical and project management skills
* Proven ability to excel in a fast-paced environment is extremely important, particularly an entrepreneurial attitude and the ability to thrive in an environment of change and ambiguity
* Experience in managing agencies and negotiating with vendors
* A positive, self-starter
* Ability to travel as needed – up to 25%

  


Experience* 10+ yrs of marketing experience, specifically with a background in B2B Field Marketing;
* Enterprise cloud software, BI or SaaS experience is an advantage.
* Have worked across EMEA North/Nordics region
* Bachelor’s degree in marketing, business management, or equivalent experience
* Partner marketing experience preferred
* Knowledge of marketing technologies such as SalesForce.com, Marketo, ON24, social platforms such as LinkedIn, and Microsoft office
* Experience of working with AI as part of marketing workflows
* Experience of working with/ability to read Intent data (6Sense preferred) at expert level
* Proven ability to excel in a fast-paced environment is extremely important, particularly an entrepreneurial attitude and the ability to thrive in an environment of change and ambiguity
* Experience in collaborating with multiple stakeholders to drive a project or program to success
* Have end-to-end, hands-on approach to marketing activities and programs


**The location for this role is:**

Hybrid: Stockholm, Sweden

**Apply now and help change how the world transforms complex data landscapes into actionable insights and turns complex data challenges into new opportunities!**
**More about Qlik and who we are:**

Find out more about ‘Life at Qlik’ on social: Instagram, LinkedIn, YouTube, and X/Twitter, and to see all other opportunities to join us and our values, check out our Careers Page.
**What else do we offer?**
* Genuine career progression pathways and mentoring programs.
* Culture of innovation, technology, collaboration, and openness.
* Flexible, diverse, and international work environment.



Giving back is a huge part of our culture. Alongside an extra “change the world” day plus another for personal development, we also highly encourage participation in our Corporate Responsibility Employee Programs

If you need assistance applying for a role due to a disability, please submit your request via email to accessibilityta@qlik.com. Any information you provide will be treated according to Qlik’s Recruitment Privacy Notice. Qlik may only respond to emails related to accommodation requests.

Qlik is not accepting unsolicited assistance from search firms for this employment opportunity. Please, no phone calls or emails. All resumes submitted by search firms to any employee at Qlik via-email, the Internet or in any form and/or method without a valid written search agreement in place for this position will be deemed the sole property of Qlik. No fee will be paid in the event the candidate is hired by Qlik as a result of the referral or through other means.
company_description: Qlik is a global software company headquartered in King of Prussia, Pennsylvania. Qlik provides an end-to-end, real-time data integration and analytics cloud platform.

title: Technical Sales Executive Scandinavia
description:
Join Nordcloud and be part of the European cloud revolution. We supercharge our customers to innovate in hyperscaler cloud, enabling seamless migration, advanced security, and data-driven success. This role focuses on winning new clients in the Enterprise and Corporate business sectors and develop some of our current customers in parallel. As a trusted advisor, you will develop strong relationships with C-level executives and stakeholders, ensuring Nordcloud becomes their go-to partner for cloud services.


**Currently, we are looking for a** **Technical Sales Executive – Scandinavia (based in Sweden)**  

This is a hybrid role and we have offices in Stockholm, Malmö and Gothenburg.

  

As a Technical Sales Executive at Nordcloud, you will be a driving force in our sales organisation, identifying client needs, designing cloud solutions, and managing the entire sales process. By delivering innovative cloud solutions, you will help clients increase operational efficiency and reduce costs, and ensuring that technology drives measurable business outcomes.


Your technical acumen and consultative approach will empower you to understand complex client challenges, architect impactful solutions, and communicate the business benefits of cloud adoption to C-level and technical stakeholders.


You will own your results, demonstrate a winner's attitude, and collaborate closely with colleagues across Nordcloud and IBM Consulting to achieve your goals.

  

**Your** **key responsibilities:**

  

* **Lead Technical Sales Engagements:** Identify client needs and design tailored cloud solutions that deliver measurable business outcomes. Drive new business within existing client base as well as within new logo acquisitions leveraging your technical expertise, solution mindset and ability to link desired business value to technical solutions.
* **Collaborate for Success:** Work as part of larger teams—often led by IBM Consulting—integrating Nordcloud's cloud solutions and value proposition into comprehensive, high-impact deals. Build strong relationships with internal teams, partners (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud), and stakeholders to deliver best-in-class solutions.
* **Create Business Value:** Apply a solution-based sales approach to address complex customer challenges, always focusing on how technology can advance the client's strategic objectives.
* **Ownership for Results:** Take full responsibility for meeting and exceeding sales targets in consultancy and service sales, with a focus on cloud infrastructure, modernization, managed services, and consulting portfolios.
* **Drive and Winner's Attitude:** Approach every opportunity with energy, resilience, and a relentless drive to win. Celebrate successes and learn from setbacks, always striving for excellence.
* **Solution Workshops & Discovery:** Lead workshops and discovery sessions with client stakeholders to uncover pain points and co-create strategies that maximize the value of cloud adoption.
* **Thought Leadership**: Represent Nordcloud at industry events and in the media, showcasing your expertise and our leadership in cloud technology.

**What we are looking for:**

  

* **Background:** Coming from a technical background with several years of experience in roles such as IT-/Cloud Architect, Cloud Engineer, IT-/Cloud CTO, IT-/Cloud Solution Manager or similar.  

**IT-/Cloud Solution Manager or similar.**
* **Experience**: 7+ years in technical sales, solution sales, IT consulting, or cloud services, with a strong technical foundation and a proven track record of winning and growing new business.
* **Solution Understanding:** Knowledge of public cloud architectures (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud), migration strategies, and cloud-native solutions. Ability to map technology capabilities to client business needs.
* **Consultative Approach:** Experience working directly with C-level stakeholders and senior IT executives, translating technical concepts into business value. With solution selling methodology you will craft tailored value propositions for complex cloud projects.
* **Ownership & Drive:** Demonstrated ability to take ownership for results, operate with a winner's attitude, and thrive in high-growth, fast-paced environments.
* **Collaboration:** Proven ability to collaborate across teams and organizations, including cloud partners, to deliver integrated solutions.
* **Communication:** Excellent communication, presentation, and organizational skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences.
* **Education:** Bachelor's degree in Business, Engineering, Computer Science, or equivalent preferred.
* **Languages:** Fluent in English and at least one Scandinavian language.
* **Bonus Points:** Hands-on experience with cloud services offerings, tools, and processes. Certifications in AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud are highly valued.

**What we offer:**

  

* Flexible working hours and hybrid working model
* Laptop and equipment to enable your efficient work
* **Local package** such as extensive private health care and insurance, wellness benefits


Please read our Recruitment Privacy Policy (https://nordcloud.com/recruitment-privacy-policy/) before applying. **All applicants must have the right to work in Sweden.**


**About Nordcloud**


Nordcloud is a European leader in cloud implementation, application development, managed services and training. It's a recognised cloud-native pioneer with a proven track record helping organisations leverage public cloud in a way that balances quick wins, immediate savings and sustainable value.
  

  

Nordcloud is triple-certified across Amazon Web Services, Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud Platform – with 10 European hubs, over 1,300 employees and has delivered over 1,000 successful cloud projects for companies ranging from midsize to large corporates.  

  

Our clients benefit from multi-cloud expertise that guides best practices, preempts pitfalls, provides essential technical support and steers teams through cultural change. From strategy planning to application management, we take our customers through the whole cloud journey to drive real business outcomes from cloud technology.  

  

Learn more at www.nordcloud.com (http://www.nordcloud.com)


*Nordcloud values diversity and is dedicated to providing equal opportunities for all candidates and employees.*
